graphdb_rdf.metric_question: What are CE marking requirements for high-risk AI systems?
graphdb_rdf.metric_answer: High-risk AI systems are required to bear the CE marking to indicate their conformity with the EU AI Act regulation. This marking allows these systems to move freely within the internal market of the EU. Specifically, the requirements are as follows: 1. **Physical CE Marking**: For high-risk AI systems that are embedded in a product, a physical CE marking should be affixed. This may be complemented by a digital CE marking. 2. **Digital CE Marking**: For high-risk AI systems that are provided only digitally, a digital CE marking should be used. Member States are also instructed not to create unjustified obstacles to the placing of these high-risk AI systems on the market. For further details, you can refer to the relevant section of the EU AI Act document (EUAIAct-1-44.pdf, page 33).
